source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure network access for Cloud Object Storage is restricted to specific IP range

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure network access for Cloud Object Storage is restricted to specific IP range
input_row.description: IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket firewall restricts all access to data unless the request originates from a list of allowed IP addresses.
input_row.rationale: Restrict access to your data stored in Cloud Object Storage buckets and enhance security controls by creating a firewall rule that will only allow data access to the list of IP addresses you specify. Impact: For any application(s) that depend upon or access data stored in Cloud Object Storage buckets to conduct its operation(s) you must ensure that the network it is using to access Cloud Object Storage buckets is also included in the COS firewall. Not doing so could possibly result in disruption of the activities.
input_row.audit: To check or confirm that an IBM Cloud Object Storage bucket is accessible only from a list of authorized IP addresses, you can navigate to Access Policies section available under your bucket information and check Authorized IPs list. 1. Go to IBM Cloud Console: https://cloud.ibm.com/resources 2. Under Storage click on the Cloud Object Storage instance you want to check ( you will be directed to a default bucket listing page ) 3. Click on the Cloud Object Storage bucket for which you want to review the bucket firewall information 4. At the bucket information screen, click on the Access policies section on the left navigation 5. Within Bucket access policies you will see the tab Authorized IPs 6. Review the list of IPs to ensure it includes the IPs you want authorized
input_row.remediation: Follow the steps outlined to add an IP to the list of Authorized IPs in bucket firewall policies. Using the console to set a firewall From the IBM Cloud console dashboard, you can restrict access to your content by setting a firewall. Set a list of authorized IP addresses 1. Start by selecting Storage to view your resource list. 2. Next, select the service instance with your bucket from within the Storage menu. This takes you to the Object Storage Console. 3. Select the bucket that you want to limit access to authorized IP addresses. 4. Select Access policies from the navigation menu. 5. Select the Authorized IPs tab. 6. Click on Add and specify a list of IP addresses in CIDR notation, for example 192.168.0.0/16, fe80:021b::0/64. Addresses can follow either IPv4 or IPv6 standards. 7. Click Add . 8. The firewall will not be enforced until the address is saved in the console. Click Save all to enforce the firewall. Note that all objects in this bucket are only accessible from those IP addresses. Default Value: By default Cloud Object Storage buckets can only be accessed by authenticated users. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage- setting-a-firewall 2. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/cloud-object-storage?topic=cloud-object-storage- setting-a-firewall#firewall-api
input_row.impact: For any application(s) that depend upon or access data stored in Cloud Object Storage buckets to conduct its operation(s) you must ensure that the network it is using to access Cloud Object Storage buckets is also included in the COS firewall. Not doing so could possibly result in disruption of the activities.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control in question relates to network access control, specifically ensuring that only specific IP ranges have access to IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets.
- Given the nature of IBM Cloud services, this type of configuration is typically exposed and manageable via APIs.
- IBM Cloud APIs provide endpoints to manage and query bucket firewall rules (as referenced in the remediation and references sections).
- The control is about access restriction, which is a standard property of network rules that can be queried and modified programmatically.
- The audit instructions specify checking a list of authorized IPs, which can be automated using IBM Cloud CLI methods or API endpoints designed for managing Object Storage access policies.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_cos_bucket_firewall_ip_restriction_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use IBM Cloud CLI or the IBM Cloud SDK to retrieve and verify the bucket access policies.
- Enumerate all IBM Cloud Object Storage buckets within the account.
- For each bucket, retrieve the current firewall settings:
  - Use the API endpoint or CLI command equivalent for accessing "Access Policies" to list "Authorized IPs".
- Validate that the IP addresses listed match the expected authorized IP ranges specified by the organization.
- Pass Criteria: The bucket's "Authorized IPs" configuration aligns with the predefined list of allowed IP addresses.
- Fail Criteria: The bucket's "Authorized IPs" configuration includes any IPs outside the allowed range or is missing expected IP ranges.
- Example command: Use `ibmcloud cos bucket-access-policies --bucket <bucket-name>` to check the firewall/IP restrictions on each bucket. 

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The task of verifying network access restrictions for IBM Cloud Object Storage can be effectively automated using IBM Cloud APIs and IBM Cloud CLI. These tools provide the necessary capabilities to programmatically retrieve and validate bucket firewall settings, making manual checks unnecessary.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The use of IBM Cloud CLI to enumerate buckets and check their access policies is valid. However, the specific command mentioned, `ibmcloud cos bucket-access-policies --bucket <bucket-name>`, should be verified for correctness and availability in the current CLI version, as CLI commands can change over time.
- The program name `ibm_cos_bucket_firewall_ip_restriction_check` is descriptive and follows a reasonable naming convention, though it could be slightly more specific by indicating the nature of the check, such as `ibm_cos_bucket_ip_whitelist_check`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- Verify the exact CLI command for checking bucket access policies, as the provided command may not exist or may have a different syntax.
- Consider using IBM Cloud SDKs for more complex automation tasks, as they might offer more flexibility and integration capabilities.
- Provide a more detailed explanation of how to handle discrepancies found during the automated checks, such as logging or alerting mechanisms.
- A better program name suggestion could be `ibm_cos_bucket_ip_whitelist_verification` to emphasize the verification aspect of the IP whitelist.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, leveraging IBM Cloud's robust API and CLI capabilities for automating compliance checks. The approach aligns with best practices for automated security audits.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify
- While the overall approach is sound, ensure the CLI command is accurate and up-to-date. Consider enhancing the program name for clarity and specificity. Additionally, provide guidance on handling non-compliance findings to complete the automation process.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions agree that this control can be audited automatically, and IBM Cloud Object Storage exposes bucket firewall (authorized IPs) configuration via API and CLI. The IBM Cloud CLI and SDKs allow programmatic retrieval of bucket firewall rules, making it feasible to enumerate all buckets and verify their authorized IP lists against a required set. Manual inspection is unnecessary as all required properties are accessible via API.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
ibm_cos_bucket_authorized_ip_restriction_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: Cloud Object Storage (COS) Buckets
- API/CLI Method: IBM Cloud CLI (`ibmcloud cos bucket-ip-access --bucket <bucket-name>`) or IBM Cloud SDK (using the `get_bucket_ip_access` or equivalent method)
- Properties to Check: List of "Authorized IPs" (IP allowlist) configured for each bucket's firewall settings
- Pass Criteria: Every bucket has an "Authorized IPs" list that matches the organization’s approved IP ranges; no bucket allows unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0) or empty IP lists.
- Fail Criteria: Any bucket is missing the "Authorized IPs" restriction, allows 0.0.0.0/0, or contains IPs outside the approved ranges.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There was no substantive disagreement between the AI opinions; both supported automation. The only minor difference was in the suggested program name. I selected a name that is both descriptive and specific to the control, following IBM naming conventions.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
